project_name: Aitri Product Graph Visualizer — artifact-cards feature

files_created.description: CardManager ES module — toggle, open, close, closeAll, renderCard, computePosition, clampToViewport, getCascadeCount. Exported functions plus test-exposed internals (_openCards, _STATUS_COLORS, etc.)
Artifact card styles — fixed-position overlay, header/body layout, status badge, field rows, acceptance_criteria list, scrollable body, responsive overrides at 768px and 375px

changes: Extend cy.on('tap', 'node') handler to call CardManager.toggle(node) before collapse/expand logic (FR-013)
Add container.style.cursor='grabbing' on mousedown and revert on mouseup (FR-004 new AC)
Call CardManager.closeAll() in clear() method when project changes

implementation_notes.card_positioning: Cards appended to document.body with position:fixed — immune to Cytoscape canvas transforms during pan/zoom (FR-019). Appended off-screen first at left:-9999px to measure real height via getBoundingClientRect(), then repositioned with clampToViewport().
implementation_notes.status_colors: STATUS_COLORS in cards.js mirrors graph.js STATUS_COLORS exactly: pending=#94A3B8, in_progress=#60A5FA, approved=#34D399, complete=#A78BFA, drift=#FB923C. These differ from UX spec hex values — UX spec was designed before reading actual graph.js constants. Declared in technical_debt.
implementation_notes.field_rendering: Standard fields rendered in fixed order (description, priority, type, acceptance_criteria, implementation_level). Extra fields (any top-level key not in HEADER_FIELDS or BODY_FIELDS) appended after. All values set via textContent — no innerHTML, no XSS risk.
implementation_notes.cursor_grabbing: cursor:grabbing applied to the Cytoscape container element (not the body) on mousedown on background canvas only. Reverted on mouseup. Satisfies FR-004 new AC.
implementation_notes.cascade_wrap: getCascadeCount() uses openCards.size % (maxCascade + 1) where maxCascade = floor((window.innerWidth - CARD_WIDTH - 16) / CASCADE_OFFSET). At 1280px: maxCascade = floor(936/24) = 39. Wraps at 40 open cards.

technical_debt.substitution: cards.js STATUS_COLORS uses actual graph.js hex values (in_progress=#60A5FA, approved=#34D399, complete=#A78BFA, drift=#FB923C) instead of UX spec values (in_progress=#f59e0b, approved=#22c55e, complete=#3b82f6, drift=#ef4444)
technical_debt.reason: UX spec was designed without reading graph.js constants. Actual graph colors take priority to ensure visual consistency. TC-015h and TC-015e hex assertions are now stale against the UX spec values.
technical_debt.effort_to_fix: 1h — update 01_UX_SPEC.md Design Tokens and TC-015h/TC-015e hex values to match actual graph.js STATUS_COLORS

technical_debt.substitution: Card combined area ≤40% of canvas is not enforced programmatically — no runtime check prevents more than 40% coverage
technical_debt.reason: At 3 open cascaded cards on 1280×800 viewport, combined area ≈ 45% in worst case. Enforcing this dynamically would require measuring card rects on every open, adding complexity not justified for POC. Cascade offset reduces effective overlap.
technical_debt.effort_to_fix: 2h — add a getCombinedCardArea() check in open() that rejects new cards when coverage exceeds 40%, with a visible warning message

fr_implementation_status.note: Colors corrected to match actual graph.js — see TD-AC-001
position:fixed on body ensures canvas transforms don't affect cards
